structures.name: 0000-Mainline

structures.name: 1000-Begin-Job
structures.description: Initializes program execution by opening input and output files and performing the first read of the load file.

structures.name: 2000-Process
structures.description: Main processing loop. Continues inserting records into the indexed file and reading the next input record until end-of-file is reached.

structures.name: 3000-End-Job
structures.description: Handles normal end-of-job processing by displaying summary statistics and closing all open files.

structures.name: 5000-Read-LOADFile
structures.description: Reads the next record from the load file, updates record counters, sets end-of-file condition, and performs error handling for read failures.

structures.name: 6000-Write-CUSTFile
structures.description: Writes a customer record to the indexed file, increments insert counters on success, and performs controlled termination on write errors.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program must read customer data sequentially from a flat file named 'customer.dat.txt' and process each record until the end of the file is reached.
assumptions: The flat file contains valid customer records formatted as expected.
Sequential reading of input file is required for data loading.

business_rules.rule_statement: Each customer record read from the input file must be inserted into an indexed customer file named 'customer.idat' with a sequential access mode.
assumptions: The indexed file is used as the target storage for customer data.
Insertion implies writing each record individually after reading.

business_rules.rule_statement: The program must maintain and display counters for the number of records read from the input file and the number of records successfully inserted into the indexed file at the end of processing.
assumptions: Counters accurately reflect successful reads and writes.
End of job display provides summary to the user.

business_rules.rule_statement: If a read operation from the input file fails (and is not end-of-file), the program must display an error message, terminate processing, and set a return code of 8.
assumptions: Any non-EOF read failure is critical and requires termination.
Return code 8 indicates a file read error.

business_rules.rule_statement: If a write operation to the indexed customer file fails, the program must display an error message, terminate processing, and set a return code of 8.
assumptions: Any write failure is critical and requires termination.
Return code 8 indicates a file write error.

rules.cobol_snippet:
OPEN INPUT LOADFile
OPEN OUTPUT CUSTFile
rules.natural_language_rule: The system initializes customer data loading by opening the source flat file for input and the indexed customer file for output.
Customer records are processed sequentially from the flat file until the end-of-file condition is reached.
Each input customer record is copied into the indexed customer file record structure before storage.
Each valid customer record read from the flat file must be written to the indexed customer file.
The system increments the flat-file read counter after each successful input record read.
The system increments the indexed-file insert counter after each successful record write.
If a flat file read error occurs (excluding end-of-file), the program must display an error message and terminate execution.
If writing to the indexed customer file fails, the program must display an error message, perform cleanup, and terminate.
At the end of processing, the system displays the total number of records read and successfully inserted.
All opened files must be properly closed before program termination.
